prompt: A SilverStage Event Ticketing & Governance system with main features including structured event setup, controlled publishing, searchable content, secure attachments, moderation, role-based accounts, and payments reconciliation for senior-focused community venues. Seniors and family members use a jQuery-driven web portal to browse seasons and sessions, view interactive seat maps by stand/zone/seat, compare ticket types (for example General Admission, Senior Discount, Companion Pass) with tiered pricing and clearly labeled sale windows such as 03/25/2026 9:00 AM–04/10/2026 5:00 PM, then reserve seats with immediate on-screen feedback when inventory is low or quotas are reached. The purchase flow supports multiple channels with quotas (e.g., 60% online portal, 40% box office) and enforces a 15-minute seat hold with automatic release; unpaid orders auto-cancel after 30 minutes. Users can search events and announcements with suggestions, highlighting, deduped results, filters like time range, author, category, and word count, and sorting by relevance/newest/popularity with paginated results. Service staff and organization admins upload supporting files (waivers, accessibility notes, event flyers) into folders with tags and version history, and can generate expiring download links (default 72 hours). Any user can report inappropriate content with a reason and evidence attachments, while moderators process cases and apply penalties such as 24-hour mutes, 7-day posting restrictions, or account bans with clear outcomes shown in the UI. The backend uses Spring MVC to expose REST-style APIs consumed by the jQuery frontend, with MyBatis handling data access and MySQL providing on-prem, offline persistence for all entities and logs. The data model must represent event → season → session → stand/zone → seat, plus ticket types with pricing rules, visibility scope, per-channel quotas, and sales start/end times; validations prevent overselling under concurrent requests by using transactional inventory deductions and unique reservation constraints. Publishing follows a workflow of draft, submission, review, and publish; post-publish corrections require an appeal record, and every change must be logged with who/when/what, provide side-by-side diff comparison, and allow rollback to any prior version within 30 days. Accounts support seniors, family members, service staff, organization admins, and platform admins under RBAC for menus and API permissions; login is strictly local username/password with at least 10 characters, complexity rules, and a 15-minute lockout after 5 failed attempts. Real-name verification is offline and manual: staff review ID information and mark status, storing sensitive fields encrypted (AES at rest) and masking display to last 4 characters. Payment and reconciliation are implemented without internet dependency by recording tenders (cash, check, or on-prem terminal batches) and importing daily settlement files from major gateways/acquirers; imported “callbacks” are processed idempotently by transaction reference, support full/partial refunds, revenue sharing between platform and merchants, and generate reconciliation and exception reports, all fully traceable through end-to-end operation logs.
